title: How to Work with a Lawyer – Client Guide
description: A practical guide for law firm clients: how to prepare for your first consultation, what to expect from working with a lawyer, and what rights you have as a client.
content:
1. Your First Meeting with a Lawyer – How to Prepare

Effective collaboration with a law firm starts with a well-prepared initial consultation. Before the meeting, gather all documents relevant to your case – contracts, correspondence, official decisions, invoices, or court rulings. The better prepared you are, the more efficiently your lawyer can assess your legal situation and propose a course of action.

For your first appointment, it is helpful to bring:

  • a valid ID (identity card or passport),
  • all written documentation related to your case,
  • a chronological summary of events written in your own words,
  • contact details of the parties involved in your dispute or transaction.

2. Legal Professional Privilege – Your Guarantee of Confidentiality

One of the fundamental rights of every law firm client is the protection of legal professional privilege. Your lawyer is strictly bound to keep confidential all information received from you in connection with your case. This obligation does not expire when your engagement ends and cannot be waived even upon request by law enforcement without a court order.

Legal professional privilege means you can freely and openly share all circumstances of your case with your lawyer – including details that may appear unfavourable to you. Only complete knowledge allows your lawyer to plan an effective defence or enforcement strategy.

3. Scope of Services and Power of Attorney

After reviewing your case, your lawyer will propose a scope of legal services covering your situation. A service agreement is typically concluded, setting out the rights and obligations of both parties. You will grant a power of attorney authorising your lawyer to represent you before courts and authorities.

Specter & Associates Law Firm clearly explains every clause of the agreement before it is signed. You have the right to ask questions, request clarifications, and negotiate the terms of your engagement.

4. Communication During Your Case

Good communication is key to efficiently handling your case. You should promptly inform the firm of any new circumstances, documents, or events related to your matter. Your lawyer, in turn, is obliged to keep you regularly updated on progress and upcoming procedural steps.

Specter & Associates Law Firm offers ongoing telephone and e-mail contact, as well as the ability to schedule consultations at any stage of the proceedings. Do not hesitate to get in touch – the more your lawyer knows, the better they can represent you.

5. Client Rights in the Lawyer-Client Relationship

As a client of a law firm, you have a number of important rights:

  • Right to information – you are entitled to be kept up to date on the status of your case and the actions taken.
  • Right to determine strategy – your lawyer is your adviser, but the final decisions on key matters (e.g. settling a dispute) rest with you.
  • Right to access your file – you may request copies of documents from your case file.
  • Right to change your representative – you may terminate your engagement with your lawyer at any time and appoint another one.
  • Right to file a complaint – if your lawyer violates professional ethics, you may lodge a complaint with the relevant Bar Association.

6. Costs and Fees

Financial matters should be discussed at the outset of your engagement. Specter & Associates Law Firm applies transparent fee arrangements – a fixed fee for the entire case, an hourly rate, or – in certain circumstances – a success fee may be agreed. All fee arrangements are documented in the service agreement.

Have questions? We invite you to an initial free consultation at our Warsaw office. Our team of lawyers is at your disposal.
